place: Somerdale borough, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Somerdale became a borough on April 23, 1929, carved out of Clementon Township alongside four other new municipalities.
The Somerdale Historical Society’s mission is to actively document and archive every significant event in the borough’s past for future generations.
In 1946, historian Martin C. published a paperback titled *Taverns, Farms, and Hamlets of Somerdale*, cataloging the town’s early life.
A time capsule containing Somerdale’s history was buried in 2019 and will be opened in 2079, offering a future glimpse into the town’s past.
Somerdale’s borders touch Cherry Hill, Gloucester Township, Hi‑Nella, Lawnside, Lindenwold, Magnolia, Stratford, and Voorhees Township.
This weekend’s Tour of Somerville race route invites drivers to explore historic sites scattered throughout Somerdale’s streets.
